institutions.name: Endangered Languages Project
institutions.blurb: Catalogue of Endangered Languages (ELCat) — collaborative documentation platform run with Living Tongues / First Peoples' Cultural Council.

institutions.name: Living Tongues Institute for Endangered Languages
institutions.blurb: Nonprofit documenting endangered languages worldwide; trains community-based linguists and runs regional director programs.

institutions.name: Wikitongues
institutions.blurb: Volunteer network recording oral histories and language-learning materials directly with speaker communities.

institutions.name: SIL International / Ethnologue
institutions.blurb: Faith-linked linguistics organization maintaining Ethnologue, the most widely cited catalogue of the world's languages.

institutions.name: Endangered Languages Documentation Programme
institutions.blurb: Grant-making programme (SOAS, London) funding fieldwork to document endangered languages.

institutions.name: Endangered Languages Archive
institutions.blurb: Digital archive (SOAS) preserving recordings, texts, and field notes from endangered-language documentation projects.

institutions.name: UNESCO World Atlas of Languages
institutions.blurb: UNESCO's global language-status platform. Caveat: per UNESCO's own pages, the interactive Atlas is offline / work-in-progress — listed as a reference pointer, not a live working tool.

institutions.name: PARADISEC
institutions.blurb: Pacific And Regional Archive for Digital Sources in Endangered Cultures — consortium of Univ. of Sydney, Melbourne, and ANU; major Pacific/PNG audio archive.

institutions.name: Museu Paraense Emílio Goeldi
institutions.blurb: Brazilian federal research museum in Belém with a long-running Amazonian indigenous-language documentation program.

institutions.name: AIATSIS
institutions.blurb: Australian Institute of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Studies — national statutory authority for Indigenous Australian language and cultural collections.

institutions.name: First Languages Australia
institutions.blurb: National peak body supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander language centres across Australia.

institutions.name: Arnold Chikobava Institute of Linguistics, Tbilisi State University
institutions.blurb: Georgia's national linguistics institute; long-standing work on Caucasian languages. Caveat: no public direct email found during verification — contact-page link only.

institutions.name: First Peoples' Cultural Council
institutions.blurb: BC Crown corporation funding and supporting Indigenous language, arts, and culture revitalization in the Pacific Northwest.

institutions.name: INALI (Instituto Nacional de Lenguas Indígenas)
institutions.blurb: Mexico's federal institute for indigenous languages — runs the national catalogue of Mexican indigenous languages and revitalization programs.

institutions.name: National Institute for Nigerian Languages (NINLAN)
institutions.blurb: Federal Nigerian institute (Aba) for research and teacher training in Nigerian languages. Caveat: TLS certificate error during live verification — phone/address corroborated only via search; recheck the cert before treating the link as a clean click-through.

institutions.name: Central Department of Linguistics, Tribhuvan University
institutions.blurb: Nepal's main academic linguistics department; documents Himalayan minority languages. Caveat: no standalone department website found — link is to a confirmed faculty/department listing page, not a dedicated institutional site.

institutions.name: Endangered Languages and Cultures of Siberia (Univ. of Surrey & SOAS)
institutions.blurb: Joint UK academic research project on Siberian indigenous languages; contact is to named research staff rather than a department mailbox.

institutions.name: Linguistics Institute, Payap University
institutions.blurb: Thailand-based linguistics institute with decades of documentation work on minority languages of mainland Southeast Asia.

institutions.name: African Academy of Languages (ACALAN)
institutions.blurb: African Union specialized agency (HQ Bamako, Mali) promoting and protecting African languages continent-wide.

institutions.name: European Language Equality Network (ELEN)
institutions.blurb: Network of 174+ member organizations across 25 European states, advocating for minority and lesser-used European languages.

institutions.name: Centre for Pacific Languages
institutions.blurb: New Zealand-based centre documenting and teaching Pacific Island languages across Oceania.

institutions.name: Society for the Study of the Indigenous Languages of the Americas (SSILA)
institutions.blurb: 501(c)(3) scholarly society (est. 1981) for the study and documentation of indigenous languages across the Americas.

institutions.name: Central Institute of Indian Languages (CIIL)
institutions.blurb: India's Ministry of Education institute running the actual government 'Scheme for Protection and Preservation of Endangered Languages.' Scoped to India only — not a stand-in for an Asia-wide body (no continent-wide Asian organization exists; this is the strongest national-level entry in that part of the world).
